title: A New Species of Pit Viper Emerges in the Himalayas
gist: DNA analysis of four snakes from Medog County, Tibet revealed a new pit viper species, raising the Ovophis genus to 10 and confirming a 2023 finding that Chinese mountain pit vipers hid unrecognized diversity.

insights.claim: A new pit viper species from Medog County in the Xizang (Tibet) Autonomous Region brings the Asian Ovophis genus to 10 known species, reported in Asian Herpetological Research.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Documents ongoing discovery of vertebrate biodiversity.

insights.claim: The four specimens showed 5.5 percent mitochondrial genetic distance from close relatives; for snakes a genetic distance of 2 to 6 percent typically warrants a potential new species designation.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: States the quantitative threshold used to define new reptile species.

insights.claim: A 2023 study found that lumping all Chinese Ovophis snakes as one species masked substantial genetic diversity, predicting hidden species now being confirmed.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Shows how genetic re-analysis splits previously single taxa.
